info.title: Nuke Vehicle Data API
info.description:
The Nuke API provides programmatic access to vehicle data management, observations,
and real-time event notifications via webhooks.

## Authentication

All API requests require authentication using either:

- **API Key**: Pass via `X-API-Key` header
- **Bearer Token**: Supabase JWT via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header

Get your API key at [https://nuke.ag/settings/api-keys](https://nuke.ag/settings/api-keys)

## Rate Limiting

API requests are rate limited. When you exceed your limit, you'll receive a 429 response.
Check the `X-RateLimit-Remaining` header to monitor your usage.

## Webhooks

Subscribe to real-time events by creating webhook endpoints. Events are delivered with
HMAC-SHA256 signatures for verification.

## External Agent Write API

`POST /v1/events` is the canonical write target for external LLM agents (Claude, ChatGPT,
bespoke MCP clients). Agents fetch the JSON Schema, prepare a strict envelope, and write
on the user's behalf — Nuke is the rails, not the destination. See the
`External Agent Write API` tag below for the full contract.

tags.name: Comparables
tags.description: Comparable vehicle sales for valuation context

tags.name: Signal
tags.description: Market signal scoring — the "is this a good deal?" endpoint. Combines comparable sales, price position, heat score, and auction sentiment into a single 0–100 deal score.

tags.name: Vision
tags.description:
Vehicle image classification and analysis powered by YONO — a locally-trained model
on Nuke's 33M-image corpus. Make classification costs $0.00/image. Full scene analysis
(category, angle, condition, camera position) uses Gemini/GPT-4o cloud fallback at
$0.0001–$0.004/image.

tags.name: Export
tags.description:
Bulk vehicle data export in CSV, JSON, or NDJSON format. Designed for data scientists,
ML pipelines, and enterprise customers. Up to 100K rows per request (service role) or
10K rows (API key) or 5K rows (user JWT).

tags.name: Exchange
tags.description:
Market exchange read API — funds, vehicle offerings, and holdings.
The Nuke market exchange enables fractional vehicle ownership backed by collector vehicle assets.

tags.name: External Agent Write API
tags.description:
Agent-facing write surface. Any LLM agent (Claude, ChatGPT, MCP client) submits structured
vehicle events on the authenticated user's behalf via `POST /v1/events`. The envelope is
strict (versioned, scope-checked, append-only); the payload is JSONB, validated against
per-event-type JSON Schemas. NUKE is rails for vehicle data — Plaid for cars — and the
schema is the moat.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of vehicles you own or have access to.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the vehicle by setting archived_at timestamp.

get.description: Retrieve observations for a specific vehicle.

post.description:
Record a new observation (data point) for a vehicle. Observations are immutable
and include full provenance tracking.

Import up to 1000 vehicles in a single request. Optionally include
observations for each vehicle.

Register a new webhook endpoint. The response includes the signing secret
which will only be shown once - save it immediately.

secret.description: Save this! Won't be shown again.

post.description: Generate a new signing secret. The old secret will be invalidated immediately.

get.description:
Returns the market signal score for a vehicle — the "is this a good deal?" answer.

Combines comparable sales, listing price vs estimated market value, heat score,
and auction sentiment into a single 0–100 deal score with confidence-weighted
signal breakdown.

Requires an existing Nuke Estimate (`nuke_estimates` row). If none exists,
call `/api-v1-valuations` first to trigger computation.

**Use cases:**
- Dealer acquisition tools ("is this worth buying at asking price?")
- Insurance platforms ("what's the market risk on this vehicle?")
- Auction bidding tools ("when to bid and when to walk away")

404.description: No signal score exists for this vehicle yet

error.example: No signal score available for this vehicle. Run a valuation first.

hint.example: Use nuke.valuations.get() to trigger a valuation, then retry.

get.description:
Returns the Nuke Estimate (valuation) for a vehicle, including confidence scoring,
deal score, and heat score. Falls back to vehicle pricing fields if no dedicated
estimate exists.

Returns external auction and marketplace listings for vehicles. Filter by
vehicle, platform (bat, cars_and_bids, etc.), or listing status.

Returns comparable vehicle sales for valuation context. Query by year/make/model
or VIN to find similar sold vehicles, with summary statistics (avg, median, min, max prices).

parameters.name: year_range
parameters.description: Years +/- from target year to include

get.description:
One-call vehicle profile by VIN. Returns core vehicle fields plus
embedded valuation, listing/observation/image counts, and first 5 images.

Paginated observation timeline for a vehicle by VIN. Each observation
includes source attribution, kind, structured data, and confidence.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filter by observation kind (e.g., listing, comment, sale, service)

get.summary: Get auction data for a vehicle
get.description:
Auction results, bid counts, recent comments, and AI sentiment analysis
for a vehicle by VIN.

Price trends by make/model/year range over a time period. Returns
time-bucketed statistics including median, percentiles, and volume.

Full-text search across vehicles, organizations, users, and tags.
Auto-detects query type (VIN, URL, year, or free text).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query — VIN, URL, year, make/model, or free text

post.description:
Classify a vehicle image using YONO — a locally-trained model on Nuke's 33M-image corpus.
Returns make, family (origin group), confidence, top-5 predictions, and vehicle detection.

**Cost: $0.00/image.** YONO runs on dedicated inference infrastructure with no cloud AI fees.

image_url.description: Publicly accessible URL of the vehicle image

top_k.description: Number of top predictions to return

family.description: Vehicle origin group (american, german, japanese, british, italian, french, swedish)

is_vehicle.description: Whether the image appears to contain a vehicle

post.description:
Full vehicle image analysis: YONO make classification + cloud scene analysis.

Returns make/family (YONO, free), plus category, subject taxonomy, condition notes,
and camera position (cloud, $0.0001–$0.004/image via Gemini or GPT-4o).

The `source` field indicates what ran:
- `yono` — YONO classification only (cloud analysis unavailable)
- `yono+cloud` — Both YONO + Gemini/GPT-4o analysis
- `unavailable` — YONO sidecar offline

image_url.description: Publicly accessible URL of the vehicle image

vehicle_id.description: Optional vehicle ID for context-aware analysis

description.description: Human-readable description of what is shown
description.example: Left front door of a Porsche 911 showing minor surface rust on lower sill

condition_notes.example: Surface rust on lower sill, paint fading on door handle

elevation_deg.description: Camera elevation relative to vehicle center

distance_mm.description: Estimated camera distance from vehicle center in mm

post.description:
Classify up to 100 vehicle images in a single request using YONO.
All classifications run locally — **cost: $0.00** regardless of batch size.

Results include per-image make, family, confidence, top-5, and is_vehicle.
Failed images are included in `results` with an `error` field.

error.description: Present only if this image failed

get.description:
Export vehicle records in CSV, JSON, or NDJSON format. Designed for data scientists,
ML pipelines, and enterprise customers.

**Row limits by auth tier:**
- Service role key: 100,000 rows/request
- API key: 10,000 rows/request
- User JWT: 5,000 rows/request

**Cursor pagination:** Use `cursor` (last vehicle `id` from previous response) for
efficient sequential pagination through large datasets.

**Partial fields:** Use `fields` to request only the columns you need — reduces
response size significantly for wide datasets.

**Parquet:** Not directly supported — export as NDJSON and convert:
`pd.read_json('data.ndjson', lines=True).to_parquet('data.parquet')`

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include. Defaults to a standard set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum rows to return (capped by auth tier)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination — vehicle `id` from last response (for sequential exports)

200.description:
Vehicle data in requested format.
- `application/json` — JSON array or wrapped object
- `text/csv` — RFC 4180 CSV with header row
- `application/x-ndjson` — one JSON object per line

next_cursor.description: Pass as `cursor` in next request for continuation. Null when exhausted.

get.description:
Returns market exchange data — funds, vehicle offerings, and holdings.

The `action` parameter controls which subset is returned:
- (none) — full snapshot: all funds + all offerings
- `funds` — all active market funds with stats
- `fund` — single fund detail (requires `symbol`)
- `offerings` — all vehicle offerings currently trading
- `holdings` — caller's holdings (requires authentication)

Stats are cached and refreshed every 15 minutes. Use `holdings` action to
get real-time position data for authenticated users.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: What to return. Omit for full snapshot.

post.description:
Same actions as GET but via POST body. Useful for actions with complex parameters
or where GET query string limits apply.

Request body mirrors GET query params: `{ "action": "fund", "symbol": "PORS" }`.

post.summary: Submit a vehicle event (agent-facing write endpoint)
post.description:
Canonical write target for external LLM agents. The agent fetches the JSON Schema,
prepares a strict envelope, and POSTs on the authenticated user's behalf.

### Envelope contract

- **Outer envelope is strict** (closed schema, additional properties rejected).
  Full schema: [envelope.json](https://nuke.ag/api/schemas/v1/envelope.json).
- **`payload` is loose** but validated against the per-event-type schema selected
  by `event_type` (`service` → service.json, `note` → note.json).
- **VIN is the canonical key.** `vehicle_ref.vin` is required; `vehicle_ref.vehicle_id`
  is optional and only used if the agent already knows Nuke's internal UUID.
- **Append-only.** Corrections submit a new event with `correction_of` pointing at the
  prior event id. The prior observation is marked `is_superseded=true`. Nothing is
  ever destructively edited.

### Routing under the hood

Submissions flow through Nuke's source-agnostic `vehicle_observations` substrate:

| event_type | observation_kind | source slug |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `service` | `work_record` | `shop` |
| `note`    | `comment`     | `agent-submission` |

Other event types (`inspection`, `modification`, `ownership_change`, `media`) are reserved
in the envelope but not yet routed; submissions for those return `400` until v1.1.

### Authentication

Required: `X-API-Key: nk_live_…` header. The key must carry a write scope that matches
the target VIN. Either of these scopes authorizes a write:

- `events:write:vehicle:{vin}` — narrow grant for one vehicle (preferred for agent integrations)
- `events:write:all` — power-user wildcard

The `auth` block inside the envelope is advisory — Nuke validates the X-API-Key header
and rejects submissions where the body's claimed scopes contradict the resolved key.

### Pilot example

See [examples/v1/mustang-session.json](https://nuke.ag/api/examples/v1/mustang-session.json)
for a full envelope: 1966 Mustang VIN `6F07C219593`, valve covers pulled, sludge in
valve galley, engine pull deferred.

mustang-service.description:
Pilot user — 1966 Mustang VIN `6F07C219593`. Pulled valve covers, found
sludge in valve galley, deferred engine pull to morning. Canonical JSON
Schemas (Draft 2020-12) for agent self-validation are mirrored at
`/api/schemas/v1/{envelope,service,note}.json`.

201.description: Event accepted and persisted as a vehicle observation.

event_id.description: Public-facing event id assigned by Nuke. Use this when submitting a `correction_of` later.

observation_id.description: Internal `vehicle_observations.id` for the row this submission produced. Same value as event_id today; reserved as a separate field in case routing changes.

vehicle_id.description: Resolved Nuke vehicle UUID for the VIN in `vehicle_ref.vin`.

observation_kind.description: How Nuke classified this submission internally (e.g. `work_record`, `comment`).

superseded_event_id.description: Set only if this submission included `correction_of` and the prior row was successfully marked superseded.

400.description: Validation failed. Envelope or payload did not match the JSON Schema.

403.description:
Authentication succeeded but the API key's scopes do not authorize a write to this VIN.
Required scope: `events:write:vehicle:{vin}` or `events:write:all`.

404.description: VIN does not resolve to a known vehicle and auto-create is not enabled for this key.

url.description: Must be HTTPS (HTTP allowed for localhost)

SignalScore.description:
Market signal score — answers "is this a good deal?"
Combines comparable sales, heat score, pricing position, and auction sentiment.

deal_score.description: Deal score 0–100. Higher is a better deal.

deal_score_label.description:
Consumer-friendly deal label.
- strong_buy: Significantly below market
- buy: Below market
- hold: At market
- pass: Above market or insufficient data
- overpriced: Meaningfully above comparable sales

heat_score.description: Market demand intensity for this vehicle class.

estimated_value.description: Estimated fair market value in USD

value_low.description: Lower bound of value range in USD

value_high.description: Upper bound of value range in USD

price_vs_market.description:
How the listing price compares to estimated market value, as a percentage.
Negative = priced BELOW market (better deal), positive = ABOVE market (overpriced).
Null if no listing price is available.

comp_count.description: Number of comparable sales used in the valuation

signal_weights.description: Contribution weights for each signal factor (0.0–1.0)

comp_coverage.description: Weight given to comparable sales coverage

condition_signal.description: Weight given to vehicle condition signals

auction_sentiment.description: Weight given to auction comment sentiment

listing_velocity.description: Weight given to market trend / listing velocity

price_position.description: Weight given to bid curve / price position signals

model_version.description: Model version used to compute the score

calculated_at.description: When the score was last calculated

is_stale.description: True if the score is older than the staleness threshold

EventEnvelope.description:
Strict envelope for `POST /v1/events`. Mirrors `docs/api/schemas/v1/envelope.json`
(the canonical Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema agents fetch for self-validation).
Closed at this layer — unknown properties are rejected so schema evolution stays explicit.

schema_version.description: Envelope schema version. Major version bumps are non-additive.

event_type.description: Discriminator selecting which payload schema applies.

vin.description:
Vehicle Identification Number. 17 chars for post-1981 vehicles; pre-1981
VINs may be shorter (e.g. classic Mustang `6F07C219593` is 11 chars).

vehicle_id.description: Optional Nuke internal UUID. The endpoint resolves VIN→vehicle_id.

occurred_at.description: When the event happened in the physical world.

submitted_at.description: When the agent assembled this submission.

session_id.description: Opaque correlation id chosen by the agent.

scopes.description:
Scopes the agent is asserting. Must include `events:write:vehicle:{vin}`
matching `vehicle_ref.vin`, or the wildcard `events:write:all`.

payload.description:
Event-type-specific body. Validated against `ServicePayload` when
`event_type=service`, `NotePayload` when `event_type=note`. Other event
types are reserved in the envelope but not yet routed.

correction_of.description:
If present, supersedes a prior event. The prior observation is marked
`is_superseded=true` and `superseded_by` points to the new row. Append-only.

submission_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 of the canonical envelope (with this field removed) for integrity.

ServicePayload.description:
Payload for `event_type=service`. Routes internally to `vehicle_observations`
with `observation_kind='work_record'`, source slug `shop`. Loose by design —
agents add fields liberally; structure hardens in v1.1 once real submissions
reveal the missing shape.

system.description: Vehicle subsystem (open enum — top_end, bottom_end, fuel, ignition, cooling, brakes, electrical, suspension, drivetrain, body, interior, ...).

NotePayload.description:
Payload for `event_type=note`. Routes internally to `vehicle_observations`
with `observation_kind='comment'`, source slug `agent-submission`. Use for
free-form remarks that don't fit a structured event type.

MarketFund.description: A Nuke market fund backed by a segment of collector vehicles

nav_share_price.description: Net asset value per share in USD

total_aum_usd.description: Total assets under management in USD

vehicle_count.description: Number of vehicles in this fund's segment

market_cap_usd.description: Total market cap for vehicles in this segment

VehicleOffering.description: A vehicle offered for fractional trading on the exchange

total_shares.description: Total shares issued for this vehicle
